To answer this question, we need to determine the object that has more thermal energy. The thermal energy of an object depends on its temperature and mass. The formula for thermal energy is:

\[
\text{Thermal Energy} = \text{mass} \times \text{specific heat capacity} \times \text{temperature change}
\]

Where:
- Mass (mass) refers to the amount of matter in the object.
- Specific heat capacity (specific heat) is the heat capacity per unit mass of the substance and determines how easily an object can be heated or cooled.
